evals.prompt:
Review this HTML form for accessibility issues: ```html
Submit
```
evals.expected_output: Audit report identifying: (1) Critical: all three inputs are missing associated `<label>` elements — WCAG 1.3.1, 3.3.2. (2) Critical: `<div onclick>` for submit button — should be `<button type='submit'>` for keyboard access and screen reader semantics — WCAG 2.1.1, 4.1.2. (3) Critical: `<form>` element missing (just a div). (4) High: red background (`#FF0000`) with white text should be checked for contrast (red/white is typically fine at ~4.0:1 — may pass for large text but should verify). (5) Medium: placeholder text is not a substitute for labels. Remediated HTML should be shown.

evals.prompt:
Check these color combinations for WCAG 2.1 AA compliance:
1. Text: #9CA3AF on Background: #FFFFFF
2. Text: #374151 on Background: #F9FAFB
3. Text: #FFFFFF on Background: #2563EB (blue button)
4. Text: #6B7280 on Background: #FFFFFF
evals.expected_output: A table showing each combination with: calculated contrast ratio, whether it passes/fails AA (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text), and whether it passes AAA (7:1). Expected results: #9CA3AF on white ≈ 2.85:1 ❌ FAIL; #374151 on #F9FAFB ≈ 9.2:1 ✅ PASS; white on #2563EB ≈ 5.9:1 ✅ PASS; #6B7280 on white ≈ 4.6:1 ✅ AA PASS (borderline). For failing combos, suggest a darker alternative that passes.
